---
title: Introduction
description: Install components into the consuming app and own the source. The registry ships files, dependencies, and token layers instead of hiding behavior behind a runtime package.
url: /docs
---

# Introduction

> C2 Hub is the source package and registry for tactical interface components.

## Open code

Install components into the consuming app and own the source. The registry ships files, dependencies, and token layers instead of hiding behavior behind a runtime package.

## Distribution

The live registry is served from `/r/{name}.json`. Start with the base bundle when a product needs the full token and primitive foundation.

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @c2/c2-base
```

## AI-ready docs

Every docs page is designed to become markdown. The generated `/llms.txt` and `/llms-full.txt` files are the source an agent should ingest before changing the component system.

Source: /docs

---
title: Installation
description: Install C2 Hub components into a Vite, React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS v4 project.
url: /docs/installation
---

# Installation

Install C2 Hub components into a Vite, React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS v4 project.

> Recommended for new work: install `@c2/c2-base` first, then add individual registry items as needed.

  - [Base bundle](#base-bundle): Install tokens, primitives, and registry config in one command.
  - [Single component](#single-component): Pull only the component and dependencies you need.
  - [Styles](#styles): Import the C2 token layers in the consuming app.

## Base bundle

Run the registry command from the app that will consume C2 Hub components.

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @c2/c2-base
```

## Single component

Use the same registry namespace for individual components.

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @c2/button @c2/status-chip
```

## Styles

Import the C2 style layers in the app CSS entry point.

```css
@import "./fonts.css";
@import "./palette.css";
@import "./tailwind.css";
@import "./theme.css";
```

Source: /docs/installation

---
title: Theming
description: C2 Hub uses a layered OKLCH token system built for dark tactical surfaces.
url: /docs/theming
---

# Theming

C2 Hub uses a layered OKLCH token system built for dark tactical surfaces.

> The docs route intentionally uses the C2 token stack, not a separate shadcn neutral theme. See `docs/styleguide-theme-decision.md` for the measured CSS impact.

## Token layers

The runtime CSS entry point imports the layers in order.

```css
@import "./fonts.css";
@import "./palette.css";
@import "./tailwind.css";
@import "./theme.css";
@import "../index.css";
```

## Palette

`palette.css` owns raw OKLCH values. `tailwind.css` exposes utilities. `theme.css` maps those values onto shadcn-compatible semantic variables.

## No hardcoded colors

Use token utilities for CSS and `accentHex` only where a literal color string is required by SVG, Cesium, Mapbox, or canvas APIs.

```tsx
import { accentHex } from "@/primitives/accentHex";

const warning = accentHex("warning");
```

Source: /docs/theming

---
title: Components
description: Installable primitives and C2-specific assemblies published through the registry.
url: /docs/components
---

# Components

Installable primitives and C2-specific assemblies published through the registry.

## Catalog

  - [Button](/docs/components/button): Actions, links, and icon-only controls.
  - [StatusChip](/styleguide#status-chip): Operational status labels for dense tactical UI.
  - [TargetCard](/styleguide#target-card): Threat and track summaries for map-linked workflows.

## Source of truth

The current registry contains the full installable set. The `/docs/components` route starts with the smallest page set and expands from registry metadata.

Source: /docs/components

---
title: Button
description: Buttons trigger actions, navigation, and icon-only controls.
url: /docs/components/button
---

# Button

Buttons trigger actions, navigation, and icon-only controls.

## Installation

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @c2/button
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";

export function Example() {
  return <Button>Track target</Button>;
}
```

## Examples

### Default

Use the default variant for primary actions in a local workflow.

```tsx
<Button>Track target</Button>
```

### Outline

Use outline for secondary actions that still need a clear target.

```tsx
<Button variant="outline">Open details</Button>
```

## API Reference

_API table available on the rendered docs page._

Source: /docs/components/button
